title: Write your business plan
description: SBA guide to traditional and lean business plan formats and common plan sections.
notes: Use as an official planning framework reference. VentureForge content remains original and should be edited for the specific business.

title: Market research and competitive analysis
description: SBA guidance on using market research and competitive analysis to understand customers, demand, market size, economics, pricing, and advantage.
notes: Use to support market-research sections and founder-led validation prompts.

title: Calculate your startup costs
description: SBA startup-cost guide for estimating launch expenses, funding needs, and break-even planning.
notes: Use alongside VentureForge editable assumptions; projections remain estimates requiring CPA/bookkeeper review.

title: Fund your business
description: SBA overview of self-funding, investors, loans, and funding tradeoffs.
notes: Use as a general funding reference. It does not guarantee funding or eligibility.

title: Loans
description: SBA overview of loan programs, lender delivery, and borrower starting points.
notes: SBA-backed loans are delivered through lenders except for disaster-related direct loans. Final decisions belong to the lender/program.

title: Lender Match
description: SBA tool for being matched with participating lenders and comparing next lending steps.
notes: Lender Match is not a loan approval. Borrowers must compare lender terms, fees, rates, collateral, and requirements.

title: 7(a) loans
description: SBA primary business loan program reference for eligible working capital, equipment, real estate, refinancing, and other business purposes.
notes: Use as the official 7(a) starting reference. SBA does not guarantee that a lender will approve a request.

title: 504 loans
description: SBA fixed-asset financing reference for eligible major assets through Certified Development Companies and lenders.
notes: Use for fixed-asset financing research only where the business model and use of funds fit.

title: Microloans
description: SBA microloan reference for smaller loans provided through intermediary lenders.
notes: Individual intermediary lenders set requirements and terms. Verify with the local intermediary.

title: Grants
description: SBA grants overview and starting point for grant-related resources.
notes: General startup grants are uncommon. Verify eligibility and deadlines on the live opportunity source.

title: Choose a business structure
description: SBA overview of business structures and why structure affects liability, taxes, registration, and operations.
notes: This is not legal or tax advice. Review structure decisions with qualified professionals.

title: Register your business
description: SBA launch guidance for registering a business after choosing name, structure, and location.
notes: Use with state/local official filing portals. Requirements vary by state and locality.

title: Get federal and state tax ID numbers
description: SBA guidance on federal and state tax ID number starting points.
notes: Do not enter SSNs or private tax IDs into VentureForge during testing.

title: Apply for licenses and permits
description: SBA starting point for identifying federal, state, and local license and permit requirements.
notes: Verify every requirement with the official federal, state, local, or professional licensing authority.

title: Open a business bank account
description: SBA guidance on business bank account preparation and typical documentation.
notes: Never enter full bank account numbers into VentureForge.

title: Get business insurance
description: SBA starting point for business insurance research and risk planning.
notes: Use with a qualified insurance agent for business-specific coverage decisions.

title: Local assistance
description: SBA local assistance locator for nearby SBA offices and resource partners.
notes: Use to find local advising and review support before major spending or applications.

title: SCORE
description: SBA resource partner offering mentoring, workshops, and small-business education.
notes: SCORE is an SBA resource partner. Availability and local services can vary.

title: Small Business Development Centers
description: SBA resource partner network for small-business counseling, planning, and technical assistance.
notes: Use local SBDC support to review assumptions, market research, financials, and launch tasks.

title: Veterans Business Outreach Center
description: SBA resource partner program for veteran and military-community entrepreneurship assistance.
notes: Use where veteran, service member, or military spouse support may be relevant.
